column1: # Input dispatch lives in the C input-binding table (callback.c action_registry +
# input_bindings; remap via `xschem bind`); the cheat-sheet takes its chords from
# `xschem bindings dump` and joins this table by id for the labels. The 'accel'
# column is a DISPLAY string only and binds nothing.
# Columns (RFC4180-style: quote any field containing a comma with "..."):
#   id        stable action key (palette + future keybinding remap)
#   menu      parent menu key == widget path under <topwin>.menubar.
#   accel     accelerator display string (NOT bound here)
#   command   Tcl command string to run (command rows only)
#   hook      proc that populates a dynamic submenu
#   help      one-line description (command palette + keybindings cheat-sheet)
#   idle      1 = the action's default key binding is idle-gated: the C dispatch
#             skips it while the editor is busy (semaphore>=2). Informational
#             mirror of the binding table's idle_only flag (`xschem bindings dump`
#             appends " idle"); per-chord truth lives in the binding table.
#   nolog     1 = do NOT push this row's command as the action log's replay
#             command for the C-backed action of the same id (action-log Layer
#             A): set when the csv command is NOT behavior-equivalent to the
#             dispatched C function. Currently only attach_labels (the key runs
# A row may have an EMPTY command: the action is C-backed and dispatched only via
# the input-binding table (callback.c action_registry); the row provides the
# label/help shown by generated views (cheat-sheet). The palette skips such rows.
